insights.anomalies: All 'longest-running' ads show identical future timestamps (April 2026), indicating a data corruption or placeholder issue in tracking. Zero 'recent signals' despite 3,889 new ads suggests either tracking lag or these new ads have generated no measurable engagement events.
insights.monthly_trends: Strong structural shift toward Education and Health verticals, which now dominate ad volume over traditional leaders like Finance. The identical timestamps on longest-running ads suggest a platform-wide reset or data anomaly affecting historical tracking dates.
insights.predicted_opportunities: Prioritize Education and Home Improvement angles, particularly vocational skills like electrician work and outdoor living projects. The high volume in these verticals indicates proven audience responsiveness and competitive but lucrative markets.
insights.today_moves: No new ads were launched in the last 24 hours, indicating a pause in testing or a consolidation phase. This suggests advertisers are analyzing performance of existing campaigns rather than introducing new creative.
insights.weekly_patterns: Massive acceleration occurred with 3,889 new ads launched in the past 7 days, representing 87% of all tracked ads. This indicates a major campaign refresh or testing wave across all verticals, especially in Education, Health, and Auto which lead in volume.
